sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Netstreit reports 2025 tax treatment of common stock distributions By Investing.com
feed.summary: Netstreit Corp. (NYSE: NTST) announced the 2025 tax treatment for its common stock distributions, classifying them as a mix of ordinary dividends and non-dividend distributions. The company issued four quarterly distributions in 2025, with 86.3772% of each classified as ordinary dividends and the remaining as non-dividend distributions, with no portion identified as capital gains or qualified dividends. All ordinary dividends qualified under Section 199A for 2025, reflecting the company's dividend growth and strong financial health despite a recent third-quarter earnings miss.

feed.title: Waste Management stock slips after Q4 earnings miss (WM:NYSE)
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) shares dropped 2% in extended trading after the company reported fourth-quarter earnings below Wall Street expectations, with adjusted earnings of $1.93 a share against an anticipated $1.95. Despite this, the company projects increased revenue, EBITDA, and free cash flow for 2026, planning to return approximately $3.5 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. Improved operating margins and cash flow in the past quarter were attributed to cost controls, operational efficiency, and investments in recycling automation and renewable natural gas.

feed.title: Axos Financial Q2 2026 earnings preview
feed.summary: This article provides an earnings preview for Axos Financial's Q2 2026 results. It focuses on the upcoming financial report for the company.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.12 EPS
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations by $0.12 with an EPS of $1.23, although revenue slightly missed targets at $3.91 billion. The stock saw a significant jump, trading at $184.47, near its 12-month high, and the company raised its quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with several firms increasing price targets.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Las Vegas Sands beats Q4 2025 forecasts, stock rises
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) exceeded Q4 2025 forecasts with an EPS of $0.85 and revenue of $3.65 billion, leading to a 2.3% stock price increase. The company highlighted strong performance at Marina Bay Sands, achieving its highest EBITDA, and growing market share in Macau’s premium segments. Executives discussed ongoing investments in property upgrades, the intense promotional environment in Macau, and Rob Goldstein's transition to a senior advisor role.

feed.title: AZZ Inc. to Participate in the Noble Virtual Equity Conference & The Barclay's Industrial Select Conference in February 2026
feed.summary: AZZ Inc. (NYSE: AZZ) announced that its Chief Marketing, Communications and Investor Relations Officer, David Nark, and Chief Financial Officer, Jason Crawford, will participate in two February 2026 conferences. They will attend Noble Capital Markets' Emerging Growth Virtual Equity Conference from February 4-5, and Barclay's 43rd Annual Industrial Select Conference from February 17-19 in Miami, Florida, holding one-on-one meetings at both events. AZZ is a leading provider of hot-dip galvanizing and coil coating solutions.

feed.title: HP Inc’s Stock Under the Microscope: Can HPQ Turn a Tepid Rally Into a Real Breakout?
feed.summary: HP Inc's stock (HPQ) has seen a cautious rally recently, with a 25-30% gain over the last year, driven by improving PC demand and AI ambitions. While analysts lean neutral to moderately positive, the stock remains below its 52-week high, suggesting potential for further upside if execution and macro conditions align. The company's strategy focuses on recurring revenue from services and AI-enabled devices, positioning it as a steady investment rather than a high-growth momentum play.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) Reports Sales Below Analyst Estimates In Q4 CY2025 Earnings, But Stock Soars 7%
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) reported Q4 CY2025 earnings with revenue of $3.91 billion, missing analyst estimates, but its non-GAAP EPS of $1.23 beat expectations. Despite a challenging macro environment with weak freight demand, the stock soared 7% following the results. The company's global forwarding segment showed growth, though overall revenue declined, and operating margins remained stable.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price: Time to Rethink Fixed- Income Diversification
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price suggests plan sponsors should rethink fixed-income diversification in 2026, moving beyond traditional stock and bond mixing. The firm advocates for incorporating private assets into target-date funds and adopting a "blend trend" that combines active and passive investment management strategies. This approach aims to address participant concerns about inflation and interest rates while offering more robust retirement investment solutions.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies awarded $62.7 million contract modification By Investing.com
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies Inc. has secured a $62.7 million contract modification from the U.S. Department of War, bringing the total contract value to $1.51 billion. This modification is for Option Year Seven of the contract, related to the Eglin Radar, Electro Optical Deep Space Surveillance Telescope, and Agile Portfolio Engineering Sustainment. Work is expected to be completed by January 31, 2027, with funding provided incrementally from fiscal 2026 operations, maintenance, and research and development funds.

feed.title: United Rentals (NYSE:URI) Misses Q4 CY2025 Sales Expectations, Stock Drops
feed.summary: United Rentals (NYSE:URI) reported Q4 CY2025 results, missing Wall Street's revenue and adjusted EPS expectations. The company's sales rose 2.8% year-on-year to $4.21 billion, falling short of estimates, and its non-GAAP profit was $11.09 per share, 6.1% below consensus. Following the announcement, the stock dropped 5.1% to $857.50, despite the full-year revenue and EBITDA guidance being close to analyst estimates.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts See Cipher Mining (CIFR) as a Buy: Should You Invest?
feed.summary: Cipher Mining (CIFR) currently holds an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.29, indicating a consensus between Strong Buy and Buy among 14 brokerage firms. However, the article advises caution against making investment decisions solely based on these recommendations due to potential positive bias from brokerage firms' vested interests. It recommends using the Zacks Rank, a proprietary stock rating tool based on earnings estimate revisions, as a more reliable indicator for near-term price performance and suggests validating ABR with Zacks Rank for better investment decisions.

feed.title: Century Communities (CCS) Projects Strong Home Deliveries for 2026
feed.summary: Century Communities (CCS) projects significant growth in home deliveries and revenue for 2026, anticipating 10,000 to 11,000 new home deliveries and revenues between $3.6 billion and $4.1 billion. The company, which focuses on single-family homes across multiple U.S. regions, exhibits strong financial health with a balanced approach to debt and stable profitability. While CCS shows strong liquidity and an Altman Z-Score indicating good financial health, its ROIC is currently below its WACC, and the real estate sector's cyclical risks suggest investors should consider volatility.

feed.title: Assurant Appoints Lynn Blake to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Assurant, Inc. announced the appointment of Lynn Blake, former Executive Vice President and Chief Investment Officer at State Street Investment Management, to its Board of Directors, effective January 28, 2026. Blake will serve on the company’s Finance and Risk Committee and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee, bringing over 30 years of experience in asset management, investment strategies, and risk management. Additionally, Debra Perry plans to retire from the Assurant Board of Directors after the 2026 annual shareholder meeting.

feed.title: Medtronic pumps $90M into Anteris TAVR program
feed.summary: Medtronic has invested $90 million in Anteris Technologies Global Corp.'s TAVR program, bringing Anteris's total new funds to $320 million. This investment follows Medtronic's focus on M&A and investment ecosystem expansion, as highlighted at a recent healthcare conference. Anteris, an Australian biomimetic heart valve company, completed a public offering to secure part of these funds.

feed.title: AkzoNobel–Axalta (AXTA) detail merger timing, $600M synergies and scale
feed.summary: AkzoNobel provided details on its planned merger with Axalta, outlining a timeline that includes a shareholder vote targeted for mid-July 2026 and a closing goal by the end of 2026, pending antitrust clearances in approximately 30 countries. The combined entity is projected to be around $15 billion, with anticipated synergies of about $600 million, primarily from procurement and functional overlaps. Management also addressed the impact on share prices, attributing recent movements to merger-arbitrage positioning rather than standalone performance.

feed.title: Equifax launches employment insights solutions for auto dealers
feed.summary: Equifax has introduced two new Employment Insights solutions for auto dealers to provide verified employment and income data during the car-buying process. These solutions integrate data from The Work Number with Equifax’s Consumer Credit Report, aiding in prequalification and financing stages to help dealers assess purchasing power and reduce buyback risk. The new offerings aim to streamline the loan approval process by moving beyond self-reported consumer information.

feed.title: United rentals posts record Q4 revenue, plans $1.5 billion share buyback
feed.summary: United Rentals (NYSE:URI) announced record Q4 2025 revenue of $4.21 billion and plans for a $1.5 billion share repurchase in 2026, alongside a new $5 billion repurchase program. Despite a 5.2% dip in net income for the quarter, the company significantly increased its quarterly dividend by 10% and reported strong operating and free cash flow for the full year. Specialty rentals showed robust growth, and the company forecasts further revenue and adjusted EBITDA growth for 2026.

feed.title: STERIS plc announces $0.63 quarterly dividend payable in March
feed.summary: STERIS plc announced a quarterly dividend of $0.63 per share, payable on March 19, 2026, continuing its 21-year streak of dividend payments. The company, which specializes in infection prevention solutions, boasts a "GREAT" financial health score from InvestingPro and an annual yield of 0.96%. This dividend announcement follows the company's Q2 fiscal year 2025 earnings report where it surpassed EPS expectations but missed revenue projections.

feed.title: Assurant taps 30-year asset manager Lynn Blake for its boardroom
feed.summary: Assurant, Inc. announced the appointment of Lynn Blake, former Executive Vice President and Chief Investment Officer at State Street Investment Management, to its Board of Directors. Blake, effective January 28, 2026, brings over 30 years of experience in asset management and will serve on the Finance and Risk Committee and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee. The company highlighted her expertise in investment strategies, risk management, corporate governance, and sustainability as valuable assets for guiding Assurant's strategic priorities.

feed.title: Expion360 reports 71% revenue growth for 2025
feed.summary: Expion360 Inc. announced preliminary unaudited financial results for 2025, reporting expected revenue of approximately $9.6 million, a 71% increase from $5.6 million in 2024. The company also improved its net loss to $6.2 million from $13.5 million the previous year and strengthened its cash position. Expion360 plans to expand into new sectors and further develop its product portfolio in 2026.

feed.title: Archer Daniels Midland hit with $40M fine in price-fixing probe, 30 years after fraud scandal
feed.summary: Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) has been fined $40 million by the SEC for allegedly misleading investors about the profits of its nutrition segment. This settlement comes over 30 years after a prior price-fixing scandal involving the company. While ADM did not admit or deny wrongdoing, former executives Vince Macciocchi and Ray Young also face civil penalties, and former CFO Vikram Luthar is charged with accounting fraud.

feed.title: Massachusetts Home Depot closed due to "significant snow" on roof, customers scramble for supplies
feed.summary: A Home Depot in Watertown, Massachusetts, was forced to close due to a "significant snow" accumulation on its roof following a recent storm, disrupting daily life in Greater Boston. This closure left many customers, including electricians and residents needing snowblowers, scrambling for essential supplies. While city crews work on cleanup, customers are driving to other locations, with one local expressing optimism about New England's winter weather despite the inconvenience.

feed.title: Fair Isaac turns in Q1 profit beat as scores revenues shine; repeats guide (FICO:NYSE)
feed.summary: Fair Isaac (FICO) reported a beat on its Q1 fiscal 2026 profit and revenue estimates, driven by strong performance in its scores segment. Despite the positive results, the company's shares dipped 2% in after-hours trading, as it reiterated its full-year 2026 guidance for adjusted EPS and revenues, which were below consensus estimates.

feed.title: (WM) Waste Management Expects 2026 Revenue Range $26.43B-$26.63B, vs. FactSet Est of $26.59B
feed.summary: Waste Management Inc. (WM) expects its 2026 revenue to be in the range of $26.43 billion to $26.63 billion. This forecast is in line with FactSet's estimated revenue of $26.59 billion for the same period. The announcement also included Q4 adjusted EPS of $1.93 per share against an estimated $1.95, and Q4 revenue of $6.31 billion against an estimated $6.39 billion.

feed.title: Trump-linked Truth Social Funds moves to buy God Bless America ETF
feed.summary: Truth Social Funds, linked to Donald Trump, is acquiring the God Bless America ETF (YALL) to expand its lineup of "America First" investment products. The acquisition aims to combine politically-oriented ETFs, offering investors options that align with their political philosophy. The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, with financial terms remaining confidential.

feed.title: Waste Management Inc Profit Climbs In Q4
feed.summary: Waste Management Inc (WM) announced a significant increase in its fourth-quarter earnings, with profit climbing to $742 million, or $1.83 per share, up from $598 million, or $1.48 per share, in the same period last year. Excluding certain items, adjusted earnings reached $780 million, or $1.93 per share. The company also reported a 7.1% rise in revenue, reaching $6.313 billion.

feed.title: MSCI, BlackRock extend long-term equity index licensing pact
feed.summary: MSCI and BlackRock have extended their ETF index licensing agreement until March 31, 2035, with automatic renewals. The revised deal involves adjusted fee schedules for certain funds based on assets under management and expense ratios, aiming to support ETF growth without significantly altering the overall fee structure. BlackRock's significant ownership of MSCI shares further highlights the strong commercial and investment relationship between the two companies.

feed.title: United Rentals Posts Record 2025 Results, Sets 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: United Rentals (URI) reported record fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results, with total revenue of $4.21 billion and rental revenue of $3.58 billion, despite margin pressures. The company also announced a positive 2026 outlook, projecting total revenue of $16.8–$17.3 billion and free cash flow of $2.15–$2.45 billion, along with a new $5 billion share repurchase authorization and a 10% dividend increase. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates URI as "Outperform," citing strong financial performance but noting risks like high debt and margin pressures.

feed.title: EXEC: VF Corp. Shares Tumble as Weak Fiscal Q1 Guidance Overshadows Q4 Earnings Beat
feed.summary: VF Corporation's shares fell after the company released weak fiscal Q1 guidance, despite reporting better-than-expected Q4 earnings driven by The North Face and Timberland. The Vans brand continued its sales decline, although the CEO expressed optimism about its turnaround, noting growth in e-commerce and positive responses to new products. Overall, the company forecasts flat to slightly up revenue for the fiscal year, with improved gross and operating margins.

feed.title: Chain Bridge Bancorp (NYSE:CBNA) Issues Earnings Results
feed.summary: Chain Bridge Bancorp (NYSE:CBNA) reported Q4 earnings of $0.81 EPS, exceeding the consensus estimate of $0.79. Despite beating earnings expectations, the stock traded down 2.7% to $32.79. Analyst ratings for CBNA include "Reduce" with a $33.00 target price, and institutional investors have recently adjusted their positions in the company.

feed.title: Home Depot cuts 800 jobs
feed.summary: The Home Depot has eliminated approximately 800 roles at its Atlanta store support center, primarily impacting its technology organization. This move coincides with the retailer mandating a five-day-a-week return-to-office policy for corporate staff, aiming to simplify operations and increase agility. The job cuts follow a third quarter where Home Depot missed profit expectations and are part of a broader trend of layoffs in the retail industry.

feed.title: Eversource roll out of ‘smart meters’ roils some Berkshire residents over possible health issues, increased fees, fire risks, privacy concerns
feed.summary: Eversource's rollout of "smart meters" in Massachusetts is facing pushback from some Berkshire residents due to concerns about potential health issues from pulsed microwave frequencies, increased electricity bills, fire risks, and privacy invasions. While Eversource touts efficiency and cost savings, state legislators are proposing bills to give consumers more rights, including the option to opt-out without penalty. The debate highlights a conflict between modernization and consumer protection, with courts also looking at the authority of local jurisdictions over telecommunications regulations.

feed.title: Tian Ruixiang Sets February 17, 2026 Shareholder Vote on Major Capital Increase and Reverse Split Authority
feed.summary: Tian Ruixiang Holdings will hold its 2026 annual general meeting on February 17, 2026, to vote on a significant increase in authorized share capital from US$200 million to US$3.75 billion and related charter amendments. Shareholders will also consider granting the board broad authority for three years to implement reverse share consolidations at various ratios. These proposals aim to provide management with flexibility to alter the company’s capital structure, with potential impacts on future financing and market compliance.

feed.title: NVIDIA Sets Conference Call for Fourth-Quarter Financial Results
feed.summary: NVIDIA announced it will host a conference call on Wednesday, February 25, at 2 p.m. PT to discuss its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2026, which ended January 25, 2026. Prior to the call, written commentary from CFO Colette Kress will be posted on investor.nvidia.com after the results are publicly announced. The webcast will be available for replay until the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 earnings call.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson beats profit outlook, misses on revenue due to lower pricing, ocean volume slump (CHRW:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) exceeded its profit expectations in the fourth quarter despite facing "significant market headwinds," leading to a rise in after-hours trading. However, the company missed its revenue targets due to lower pricing and a significant slump in ocean freight volumes. The challenging macro environment impacted global freight demand and increased spot costs.

feed.title: Resources Connection declares $0.07 dividend
feed.summary: Resources Connection, Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of $0.07 per share. The dividend will be payable on March 20, 2024, to shareholders of record as of February 26, 2024.

feed.title: AIP Corp, Caterpillar form alliance to deploy 2 GW power for AI data centers
feed.summary: American Intelligence & Power Corporation (AIP Corp) has partnered with Caterpillar Inc. and Boyd CAT to deploy 2 gigawatts of natural gas generator sets for its Monarch Compute Campus in West Virginia, specifically to power AI data centers. This strategic alliance includes a purchase agreement for generators with advanced emissions controls, integrated with battery energy storage systems, to address the variable load demands of AI operations. Caterpillar's involvement is supported by its strong financial performance and its ability to provide vendor equipment financing for the project.

feed.title: Qorvo and Skyworks Announce Merger Agreement
feed.summary: Qorvo, Inc. has announced a merger agreement with Skyworks Solutions, Inc., involving a two-step process where Qorvo will merge with Skyworks' subsidiaries. Skyworks filed a registration statement for the merger on December 4, 2025, and special stockholder meetings are scheduled for February 11, 2026. The merger is facing two lawsuits challenging it due to alleged disclosure deficiencies.

feed.title: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q4
feed.summary: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) reported better-than-expected revenue in Q4 CY2025, with sales up 22.1% year-on-year to $5.34 billion, surpassing analyst estimates. The company also provided surprisingly strong revenue guidance for Q1 CY2026, projected at $5.7 billion at the midpoint, exceeding analyst expectations. This performance, along with solid adjusted EPS and operating income, caps a record year for the semiconductor equipment maker, demonstrating sustained growth and effective inventory management.

feed.title: IBM (NYSE:IBM) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q4 CY2025, Stock Soars
feed.summary: IBM reported strong Q4 CY2025 results, significantly beating Wall Street's revenue and adjusted EPS expectations, with sales up 12.1% year-on-year to $19.69 billion. The company's performance was driven by double-digit growth in Software and Infrastructure, alongside a generative AI book of business exceeding $12.5 billion. IBM's stock surged 7.9% post-announcement, and the company issued optimistic guidance for 2026, forecasting over 5% constant currency revenue growth.

feed.title: WM Announces Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Earnings
feed.summary: WM announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year 2025, driven by operational excellence and strategic investments. The company delivered record performance in operating expenses as a percentage of revenue and saw significant growth in cash flow from operations and adjusted operating EBITDA. WM expects continued growth in 2026, including a nearly 30% increase in free cash flow, and plans to return approximately $3.5 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands Logs Higher Fourth-Quarter Profit, Revenue
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands reported higher profit and revenue in its latest quarter, driven by strong performance at its Singapore resort. The company posted a profit of $395 million, or 58 cents per share, on revenue of $3.65 billion, exceeding analyst estimates. Despite the positive financial results, the stock slid 10.6% in after-hours trading, though shares had climbed 42% over the previous 12 months.

feed.title: Southwest: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines Co. (LUV) reported a fourth-quarter profit of $323 million, or 61 cents per share, which adjusted to 58 cents per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations. However, the airline's revenue of $7.44 billion fell short of analysts' forecasts. Southwest anticipates earnings of 45 cents per share for the current quarter and $4 per share for the full year.

feed.title: AAR celebrates Airframe MRO expansion in Oklahoma City, prepares to induct additional Alaska Airlines aircraft
feed.summary: AAR CORP. has completed a significant expansion of its Airframe MRO facility in Oklahoma City, adding three maintenance bays for 737 variants. This expansion, driven by increased demand, will enable AAR to service additional Alaska Airlines aircraft as part of a long-term commitment. A ribbon-cutting ceremony marked the occasion, celebrating the creation of 200 new jobs and future digitization efforts in collaboration with Alaska Airlines.

feed.title: New AAR aircraft maintenance hub brings 200 careers to Oklahoma City
feed.summary: AAR has completed a significant expansion of its Airframe MRO facility in Oklahoma City, adding over 80,000 sq ft and three maintenance bays capable of servicing all 737 variants. This expansion creates 200 new full-time jobs and supports a long-term commitment with Alaska Airlines, including the induction of additional aircraft and the digitization of maintenance processes. The facility's operations in Oklahoma City span over 50 years.

feed.title: AAR Expands MRO Services in Oklahoma City with New Maintenance Bays for 737 Aircraft
feed.summary: AAR CORP has completed a significant expansion of its Airframe MRO facility in Oklahoma City, adding over 80,000 square feet and three new maintenance bays for Boeing 737 aircraft. This expansion aims to meet increasing demand for MRO services and strengthens AAR's long-standing partnership with Alaska Airlines, which will induct more aircraft into the facility. The initiative also created 200 new jobs and includes plans for modernizing maintenance with digitization and advanced technologies.

feed.title: Resources Connection Inc - On Jan 22, Authorizes Reduction Of Global Management & Administrative Workforce
feed.summary: Resources Connection Inc. announced on January 22nd that it has authorized a reduction of its global management and administrative workforce. The brief report from Refinitiv and Reuters did not provide further details on the scope or reasons for the workforce reduction.

feed.title: Toro (NYSE:TTC) Reaches New 1-Year High - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Toro (NYSE:TTC) recently hit a new 52-week high of $91.05 and is currently trading at $89.95, following news of beating quarterly earnings estimates and setting positive FY2026 guidance. Despite this, analysts have a consensus "Hold" rating on the stock with an average target price of $92.20. The company also increased its quarterly dividend and is largely institutionally owned.

feed.title: Shift5 hires former L3Harris exec as interim CEO
feed.summary: Shift5, a cybersecurity startup, has appointed Toby Magsig, formerly of L3Harris Technologies, as its interim chief executive. Magsig takes over from co-founder Josh Lospinoso, who will transition to focus on long-term planning while remaining on the board of directors. Shift5, known for its operational technology products for defense and transportation, recently secured a $75 million Series C funding round.
